description: Search message content across all channels accessible to the user, with pluggable search provider support and real-time result streaming

actors.name: Searching User
actors.description: Authenticated user performing a content search across messages

actors.name: Search Provider
actors.description: Pluggable backend service that indexes messages and processes search queries; may be built-in or an external search engine

actors.name: Index Worker
actors.description: Background process that keeps the search index up to date as messages, users, and channels change

general: The searching user must be authenticated
A search provider must be active and configured; if no provider is active, search is unavailable
Search results are filtered to channels and messages the searching user has permission to access; no results from inaccessible channels are exposed
When a channel_id is provided, results are scoped to that channel only
Search supports pagination through an opaque page token passed in each subsequent request
The search provider is pluggable; the system exposes a standard interface so providers can be swapped without API changes
Provider settings (index size, stemming, language, etc.) are managed through the provider's own configuration, not the search API
Suggestion queries are distinct from result queries; a provider may support suggestions independently
The index is kept current via event-driven updates: user save/delete and channel save/delete events are forwarded to the index worker
Search results include message documents; providers may optionally include user and channel records in the same result set
The result format follows a standard envelope with start offset, total found count, and document array for each entity type
Search operations are asynchronous; the system waits for the provider to resolve before returning results
Errors from the provider (timeouts, index unavailability) are surfaced as search errors, not silent failures

user_not_authenticated.result: Search rejected; user must be logged in

given: no search provider is currently active or configured
no_provider_active.result: Search rejected; the search service is not available

empty_query.result: Search rejected; a non-empty search term is required

given: search provider returns an error or times out
provider_error.result: Search failed; the search service encountered an error

given: query_text is a partial term suitable for autocomplete
suggestions_returned.result: Autocomplete suggestion list is returned to the client for display

results_returned.result: Matching messages are returned ordered by relevance; users and rooms included if provider supports them

no_results.result: Empty result set returned; user sees a no-results message

errors.message: You must be logged in to search messages
Message search is not available at this time
Search encountered an error; please try again

events.name: search.completed
events.description: Fired when a search query returns results (including empty results)

events.name: search.suggestions_returned
events.description: Fired when autocomplete suggestions are returned for a partial query

related.reason: Search spans direct messages and channel messages accessible to the user
Pinned messages are indexed and discoverable via search
Users may use search to find content they previously starred
Shared file messages with descriptions are included in search results

goals.description: Return relevant message search results scoped strictly to channels the searching user has permission to access

success_metrics.measurement: Search results limited to accessible channels / total results returned
Search requests served successfully / total requests when provider is active

human_checkpoints: before switching the active search provider
before reindexing the entire message corpus

invariants: results never include messages from channels the user cannot access
search is unavailable when no provider is active
empty query always returns SEARCH_EMPTY_QUERY error

acceptance_tests.given: user has access to channel A but not channel B
acceptance_tests.when: search returns messages from both channels
acceptance_tests.expect: only channel A messages are included in results

capabilities.description: Query pluggable search provider and filter results to channels accessible by the requesting user
Support paginated retrieval of search results via opaque page tokens

tradeoffs.reason: access filtering is mandatory even if it means returning fewer results than the provider found
